text:
seeking applicability of Section 18 of the Limitation Act.
c) 
The communication dated 04.02.2015 never admitted the 
liability/debt. In fact, it clearly denied the liability/demand on 
the ground that there could be no retrospective revision of 
tariff. It is, thus, wrong on the part of the appellant to argue 
that the communication dated 04.02.2015 acknowledged the 
liability/debt.
d) 
The demands raised prior to 12.08.2015 were not under any 
statutory provision. These demands were being raised by the 
lessor to the lessee. The statutory authority under the PP Act for 
the first time, issued show cause notice on 12.08.2015, which 
admittedly was beyond a period of three years if the benefit of 
section 18 of the Limitation Act is not extended. Had there been 
a notice under Section 7(3) of the PP Act prior to 11.05.2015 
to which a reply had been given by the respondents may be 
for deferment of the demand in view of the pending appeal
